title: Gendos Reaction
wikitext:
[]: Gendo's Reaction             Quote Post by gerry_dgk » Wed Nov 18, 2009 7:40 pm Remember when Gendo opened the super heated hatch in order to save Rei. I had this questioned asked to me. Why did he go to save her if he could just rebuild her?? I want your thoughts and the reasons you think. Hahaha yeah just clone her or whatever. I said rebuild cause in the chamber when Ritsuko took Shinji too see the truth he called the others spare parts.

[]: Re: Gendo's Reaction             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Nov 18, 2009 7:44 pm gerry_dgk wrote: Why did he go to save her if he could just rebuild her??

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Nov 18, 2009 7:46 pm if she was rebuilt... that would be some freaky frankenstein crap there

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Nov 18, 2009 7:51 pm he cares about her. i don't remember exactly but when she ran in one-armed with an N2 mine was he upset about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Nov 18, 2009 7:58 pm On a less pet the dog view, she was possibly the only viable pilot at the time (depending on how he considered Asuka and if he planned to use Shinj from the beginning)

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:30 pm Gendo saved her because he cares about her. If he didn’t care there’s no way he would have burned his hands to save her.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:50 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3417

[]: Gendos reaction             Quote Post by superfreak110 » Tue Nov 24, 2009 6:47 pm When Gendo opened that hatch he also most likely saw Rei as 1. his wife because she is more or less a clone of Yui and 2. She was the key to the Instrumentality project in the End of Evangelion.

[]: Re: Gendos reaction             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Nov 24, 2009 6:55 pm superfreak110 wrote: 1. his wife because she is more or less a clone of Yui and 2. She was the key to the Instrumentality project in the End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Tue Nov 24, 2009 8:02 pm Gendo saved Rei to keep Lilith's soul aligned with him and his own plan for Third Impact. There is also the fact that Rei is the salvaged remains of Yui.

[]: Re: Gendos reaction             Quote Post by questforsteve » Thu Nov 26, 2009 10:12 pm superfreak110 wrote: 2. She was the key to the Instrumentality project in the End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 26, 2009 10:33 pm Gendo has feelings too. At this particular moment, his emotions for both Rei and what she represented kicked in. Just because he's a douche doesn't mean that he's one all the time. Besides, it must be a pain in the ass to go through the process of replacing the soul into another body.

[]:             Quote Post by questforsteve » Thu Nov 26, 2009 10:38 pm Sachi wrote: Gendo has feelings too. At this particular moment, his emotions for both Rei and what she represented kicked in. Just because he's a douche doesn't mean that he's one all the time. Besides, it must be a pain in the ass to go through the process of replacing the soul into another body.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Fri Nov 27, 2009 2:52 pm questforsteve wrote: A very good point indeed, sir. I had thought about that "getting caught in the moment" idealism idealism for some time myself and still consider it a viable way to explain his reaction. As for moving the souls, yes it probably is hard, but I don't know, I've never tried; and hopefully niether have any of you readers. That would be kind of creepy.

[]:             Quote Post by gerry_dgk » Tue Dec 08, 2009 5:42 am This has gotten pretty deep. Maybe should have gone into discussion instead. But bravo all of you. And I too maybe his feelings leaked out a little and he got caught up in the moment

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Sat Dec 12, 2009 8:46 pm He needed an excuse to wear gloves, duh. The "Hellsing cosplay" excuse wasn't going to cut it.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Dec 13, 2009 5:04 am Why cant people shrug off the fact that he just did it out of impulse? I am so very very doubtful they thought at that point of the whole adam-embryo-grafted-into-hand-to-fondle-Rei. Or that he didn't actually care for Rei, he was just trying to do something drastic in order to gain her unconditional trust. Not everything in the show has to have reasoning behind it. This is one of those cases imo.

[]:             Quote Post by zilluss » Sun Dec 13, 2009 8:46 am I think it's because the relationship between the two is not within the soul but within Rei II. Thus, the new Rei would be a "known stranger" to Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Dec 13, 2009 7:29 pm toe mash wrote: Why cant people shrug off the fact that he just did it out of impulse? I am so very very doubtful they thought at that point of the whole adam-embryo-grafted-into-hand-to-fondle-Rei. Or that he didn't actually care for Rei, he was just trying to do something drastic in order to gain her unconditional trust. Not everything in the show has to have reasoning behind it. This is one of those cases imo.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Dec 14, 2009 4:17 am Given how often Anno focuses on hands as symbolic of free will, I think it's supposed to be pretty telling that Gendou's hands are covered for the majority of the series.
